title: Remotes, Tracking, Fetch, Pull, and Push · Mastery Check
summary: Synchronize with a shared repository while separating network transfer, local integration, authentication, and authorization decisions. Every item uses a fresh case and misconception-specific distractors. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for a remote synchronization trace and collaboration runbook. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Distinguish remote names, URLs, remote-tracking references, local branches, upstreams, and push destinations.
Fetch explicit remotes and refspecs, inspect updates, and compare local work before integrating.
Explain pull as fetch plus configured integration and select merge, rebase, or fast-forward-only deliberately.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# git-basics cumulative repository evidence notebook
# Git operations change only disposable simulated repository state.
# Inspect status, diffs, graph, refs, and recovery before every mutation.

steps.title: Reconstruct the defensible process
steps.instruction: Order the Remote Reference Model evidence cycle for this quiz task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Remote Reference Model assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Local main is 5 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Remote Reference Model has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Remote Reference Model precondition or invariant that the quiz case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Local main is 5 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: origin is a special central repository that Git discovers automatically.

options.text: Inspect repository boundary, config origin, current operation, worktree, index, refs, and graph
Preserve uncommitted work and a recovery ref, then state intended tree and topology
Preview and perform the least destructive simulated Git operation
Verify status, staged and range diffs, graph, tests, remote target, and recovery path

stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A successful SSH connection proves permission to push every branch.

lines.label: required

options.text: Apply the visible technique immediately, omit assumptions, and treat one successful output as complete proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision
steps.instruction: Write the quiz evidence note for Credential Transport: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Local main is 2 commits ahead and one behind origin/main; fetch updates only remote-tracking refs. Compare ranges, choose integration policy, and name exact source/destination before push.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate HTTPS or SSH transport, credentials, host trust, authorization, signing, and repository policy.

checks.description: The evidence cycle runs from problem framing through changed-case validation.
checks.failureMessage: Frame the decision before modeling, and validate after obtaining a result.

checks.failureMessage: Recheck the conditions and reject the documented misconception about Remote Reference Model.
